content.problemStatement:
In a red-blue tree, each vertex is either red or blue and adjacent vertices always have different colours.

You are given a tree with NNN vertices (numbered 111 through NNN). It is not necessarily a red-blue tree, but its vertices are still coloured red and blue. You may perform the following operation any number of times (including zero): choose two adjacent vertices and swap their colours.

Find the smallest number of operations required to transform the tree into a red-blue tree or determine that it is impossible.
constraints: the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10610^6106
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
N−1N-1N−1 lines follow. Each of these lines contains two space-separated integers uuu and vvv denoting that vertices uuu and vvv are connected by an edge.
The last line contains a string SSS with length NNN. For each valid iii, the iii-th character of this string is either '0' if the iii-th vertex is initially red or '1' if it is initially blue.
content.outputFormat: Print a single line containing one integer ― the smallest number of operations or −1-1−1 if it is impossible to transform the tree into a red-blue tree.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: We can perform the following operations:

Swap the colours of vertices 111 and 333; the string of colours becomes "1000010".
Swap the colours of vertices 111 and 222; the string of colours becomes "0100010".
Swap the colours of vertices 666 and 333; the string of colours becomes "0110000", so the tree is a red-blue tree.
